backtrace.c(3) Library Functions Manual backtrace.c(3)

NAME

backtrace.c - 📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting implementation

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t *bt)
Capture current stack into bt. void backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
Symbolize bt->ptrs into bt->symbols. void backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t *bt)
Capture and symbolize in one call. void backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t *bt)
Free backtrace symbols. void backtrace_print (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
Print backtrace with dual output: colored stderr + plain log file. void backtrace_print_many (const char *label, const backtrace_t *bts, int count)
Print multiple backtraces. int backtrace_format (char *buf, size_t buf_size, const char *label, const backtrace_t *bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
Format backtrace to buffer (plain ASCII, no colors). void backtrace_log_frames (int num_frames, int skip_frames)
Log N backtrace frames with function name, line number, and file. void backtrace_print_symbols (const char *label, char **symbols, int count, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
Print pre-resolved backtrace symbols with consistent formatting.

Detailed Description

📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting implementation

Definition in file debug/backtrace.c.

Function Documentation

void backtrace_capture (backtrace_t * bt)

Capture current stack into bt. Calls platform_backtrace() to capture raw return addresses. Does not symbolize (symbols remain NULL).

Parameters

bt Backtrace structure to fill (must not be NULL)

Definition at line 17 of file debug/backtrace.c.

17                                         {
18   if (!bt) {
19     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace");
20     return;
21   }
22   bt->count = platform_backtrace(bt->ptrs, 32);
23 }




References backtrace_t::count, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    platform_backtrace(), backtrace_t::ptrs, and
  SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by backtrace_capture_and_symbolize().






void
  backtrace_capture_and_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Capture and symbolize in one call. Convenience function combining
    backtrace_capture() and backtrace_symbolize().




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure to fill and symbolize (must
  not be NULL)


Definition at line 36 of file debug/backtrace.c.


36                                                       {
37   if (!bt) {
38     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
39     return;
40   }
41   backtrace_capture(bt);
42   backtrace_symbolize(bt);
43 }






References backtrace_capture(),
    backtrace_symbolize(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by asciichat_fatal_with_context(), and
    backtrace_log_frames().






int
  backtrace_format (char * buf, size_t buf_size, const char * label, const
  backtrace_t * bt, int skip_frames, int max_frames,
  backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)


Format backtrace to buffer (plain ASCII, no colors). Formats a
    backtrace into a plain ASCII buffer without ANSI color codes. This is the
    safe choice for:


    
  
  • Generating log file content
    • 
  
  • Embedding in error messages or email alerts
    • 
  
  • Storing in databases or structured logs
    • 
  
  • Sending over network protocols
    • 



The buffer must not be freed before use - the returned count is
    the number of bytes written. The buffer is null-terminated if space
  permits.








Output
  Example


char buffer[2048];
backtrace_t bt;
backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&bt);
int written = backtrace_format(buffer, sizeof(buffer),


                               "Crash", &bt, 0, 10, NULL);
if (written > 0) {


    // Safe to use with logging system


    log_error("Program crashed:\n%s", buffer);


    // Safe to include in structured fields


    json_add_string(crash_report, "backtrace", buffer);
}


Parameters


buf Output buffer (will be null-terminated if
  space permits)


buf_size Size of output buffer in bytes (must be at least 1)


label Header label (e.g., "Crash", "Assertion")


bt Backtrace to format (must not be NULL, must be symbolized)


skip_frames Number of frames to skip from start (0 = all)


max_frames Maximum frames to include (0 = unlimited)


filter Optional filter callback (NULL = no filtering)


Returns


Number of bytes written (not including null terminator),
  or -1 on error If buffer is too small, returns the bytes that would be written
  if space allowed


Note


Buffer is always null-terminated on success (output <
  buf_size)



This is the preferred output method for integration with
    structured logging




No ANSI color codes are included, making output safe for any
    destination




See also


backtrace_print() for dual (colored terminal +
  plain log file) output


Definition at line 347 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


348                                                                       {
349   if (!buf || buf_size == 0 || !bt || !bt->symbols || bt->count <= 0) {
350     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
351     return -1;
352   }
353 
354   // Capture count and symbols once to avoid TOCTOU race with other threads
355   int count = bt->count;
356   char **symbols = bt->symbols;
357 
358   int offset = 0;
359 
360   // Add label
361   offset += safe_snprintf(buf + offset, buf_size - (size_t)offset, "%s\n", label);
362 
363   // Calculate frame limits
364   int start = skip_frames;
365   int end = count;
366   if (max_frames > 0 && (start + max_frames) < end) {
367     end = start + max_frames;
368   }
369 
370   // Add frames
371   int frame_num = 0;
372   for (int i = start; i < end && i < count && offset < (int)buf_size - 128; i++) {
373     // Safely access symbols[i] - symbols array might be smaller than count or have NULL entries
374     const char *symbol = NULL;
375     if (symbols && symbols[i]) {
376       symbol = symbols[i];
377     } else {
378       symbol = "???";
379     }
380 
381     // Skip frame if filter says to
382     if (filter && filter(symbol)) {
383       continue;
384     }
385 
386     offset += safe_snprintf(buf + offset, buf_size - (size_t)offset, "  [%d] %s\n", frame_num, symbol);
387     frame_num++;
388   }
389 
390   return offset;
391 }






References backtrace_t::count, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    safe_snprintf(), SET_ERRNO, and
  backtrace_t::symbols.






void
  backtrace_print (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bt, int
  skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)


Print backtrace with dual output: colored stderr + plain log file.
    Intelligently formats and routes backtraces to multiple destinations:


    
  
  • stderr: Colored output with semantic highlighting (for interactive
      debugging)
    • 
  
  • log file (if logging): Plain ASCII without ANSI codes (for file
      parsing)
    • 



This dual-output design ensures readable terminal output during
    development while keeping log files clean and searchable in production.








Output
  Format


Terminal output example:




=== Backtrace: Error ===
[0] 0x7f1234567890 in main() [src/main.c:42]
[1] 0x7f1234567800 in process_request() [src/network.c:128]
[2] 0x7f1234567700 in send_data() [src/io.c:67]



Log file (same content, no colors):




=== Backtrace: Error ===
[0] 0x7f1234567890 in main() [src/main.c:42]
...



Parameters


label Header label (e.g., "Backtrace",
  "Leak trace", "Assertion failure")


bt Backtrace to print (must not be NULL, must be symbolized via
  backtrace_capture_and_symbolize)


skip_frames Number of frames to skip from start (0 = include all, 1 =
  skip first frame, etc.)


max_frames Maximum frames to print (0 = unlimited, 5 = first 5 frames
  only)


filter Optional callback to selectively skip frames (e.g., filter
  internal frames) Return true to skip frame, false to include. NULL = no
  filtering.


Note


Backtrace must be symbolized. Unsymbolized (raw hex)
  traces are less useful.



Frame filtering is useful to focus on application code,
  excluding:


    
  
  • lib/platform internal calls
    • 
  
  • lib/log infrastructure
    • 
  
  • Exception handling wrappers
    • 





See also


backtrace_format() for buffer-based output (safe
  for log system)


Definition at line 56 of file debug/backtrace.c.


57                                                       {
58   if (!bt || !bt->symbols || bt->count <= 0) {
59     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
60     return;
61   }
62 
63   // Capture count and symbols once to avoid TOCTOU race with other threads
64   int count = bt->count;
65   char **symbols = bt->symbols;
66 
67   // Calculate frame limits
68   int start = skip_frames;
69   int end = count;
70   if (max_frames > 0 && (start + max_frames) < end) {
71     end = start + max_frames;
72   }
73 
74   // Build backtrace in two versions: colored for terminal, plain for log file
75   char colored_buffer[16384] = {0};
76   char plain_buffer[16384] = {0};
77   int colored_offset = 0;
78   int plain_offset = 0;
79 
80   // Format log header using logging system's template
81   char log_header_buf[512] = {0};
82   time_t now = time(NULL);
83   struct tm *tm_info = localtime(&now);
84   char timestamp[32];
85   strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%H:%M:%S", tm_info);
86 
87   thread_id_t tid = asciichat_thread_self();
88   uint64_t tid_val = (uintptr_t)tid;
89 
90   // Get current time in nanoseconds for template formatting
91   uint64_t time_ns = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() * 1000ULL;
92 
93   // Try to format header using the logging system's template with color
94   log_template_t *format = log_get_template();
95   if (format) {
96     // Color the label with WARN color for terminal output
97     const char *colored_label_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, label);
98     char colored_label_buf[256];
99     strncpy(colored_label_buf, colored_label_ptr, sizeof(colored_label_buf) - 1);
100     colored_label_buf[sizeof(colored_label_buf) - 1] = '\0';
101 
102     int len = log_template_apply(format, log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), LOG_WARN, timestamp, __FILE__,
103                                  __LINE__, __func__, tid_val, colored_label_buf, true, time_ns);
104     if (len > 0) {
105       // Successfully formatted with logging template
106       colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
107                                       "%s\n", log_header_buf);
108     } else {
109       // Fallback: manual formatting if template fails
110       safe_snprintf(log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), "[%s] [WARN] [tid:%llu] %s: %s", timestamp, tid_val,
111                     __func__, label);
112       const char *colored_header_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, log_header_buf);
113       char colored_header_buf[512];
114       strncpy(colored_header_buf, colored_header_ptr, sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1);
115       colored_header_buf[sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1] = '\0';
116       colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
117                                       "%s\n", colored_header_buf);
118     }
119   } else {
120     // Fallback: manual formatting if no template available
121     safe_snprintf(log_header_buf, sizeof(log_header_buf), "[%s] [WARN] [tid:%llu] %s: %s", timestamp, tid_val, __func__,
122                   label);
123     const char *colored_header_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, log_header_buf);
124     char colored_header_buf[512];
125     strncpy(colored_header_buf, colored_header_ptr, sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1);
126     colored_header_buf[sizeof(colored_header_buf) - 1] = '\0';
127     colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
128                                     "%s\n", colored_header_buf);
129   }
130 
131   // Add plain label header for log file
132   plain_offset +=
133       safe_snprintf(plain_buffer + plain_offset, sizeof(plain_buffer) - (size_t)plain_offset, "%s\n", label);
134 
135   // Build backtrace frames with colored output for terminal, plain for log
136   int frame_num = 0;
137   for (int i = start; i < end && i < count && colored_offset < (int)sizeof(colored_buffer) - 512; i++) {
138     // Safely access symbols[i] - symbols array might be smaller than count or have NULL entries
139     const char *symbol = NULL;
140     if (symbols && symbols[i]) {
141       symbol = symbols[i];
142     } else {
143       symbol = "???";
144     }
145 
146     // Skip frame if filter says to
147     if (filter && filter(symbol)) {
148       continue;
149     }
150 
151     // Build frame number string
152     char frame_num_str[16];
153     safe_snprintf(frame_num_str, sizeof(frame_num_str), "%d", frame_num);
154 
155     // Get colored frame number - copy to temp buffer to avoid rotating buffer issues
156     const char *colored_num_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, frame_num_str);
157     char colored_num_buf[256];
158     strncpy(colored_num_buf, colored_num_ptr, sizeof(colored_num_buf) - 1);
159     colored_num_buf[sizeof(colored_num_buf) - 1] = '\0';
160 
161     // Parse symbol to extract parts for selective coloring
162     // Format: "[binary_name] function_name() (file:line)"
163     char colored_symbol[2048] = {0};
164     const char *s = symbol;
165     int colored_sym_offset = 0;
166 
167     // Color binary name between brackets
168     if (*s == '[') {
169       colored_sym_offset +=
170           safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "[");
171       s++;
172       // Find closing bracket
173       const char *bracket_end = strchr(s, ']');
174       if (bracket_end) {
175         int bin_len = bracket_end - s;
176         char bin_name[512];
177         strncpy(bin_name, s, bin_len);
178         bin_name[bin_len] = '\0';
179         const char *colored_bin_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, bin_name);
180         char colored_bin_buf[512];
181         strncpy(colored_bin_buf, colored_bin_ptr, sizeof(colored_bin_buf) - 1);
182         colored_bin_buf[sizeof(colored_bin_buf) - 1] = '\0';
183         colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
184                                             sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s", colored_bin_buf);
185         colored_sym_offset +=
186             safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "] ");
187         s = bracket_end + 1;
188       }
189     }
190 
191     // Skip leading spaces after bracket
192     while (*s && *s == ' ')
193       s++;
194 
195     // Parse: could be "function() (file:line)" or "file:line (unresolved)"
196     const char *paren_start = strchr(s, '(');
197 
198     // Detect format: if there's a colon before the first paren, it's "file:line (description)"
199     const char *colon_pos = strchr(s, ':');
200     if (colon_pos && paren_start && colon_pos < paren_start) {
201       // Format: "file:line (unresolved function)" - rearrange to "(unresolved) (file:line)"
202       // Extract file:line part (trim trailing spaces)
203       int file_len = paren_start - s;
204       while (file_len > 0 && s[file_len - 1] == ' ')
205         file_len--;
206       char file_part[512];
207       strncpy(file_part, s, file_len);
208       file_part[file_len] = '\0';
209 
210       // Extract description content (without parens)
211       const char *paren_end = strchr(paren_start, ')');
212       int desc_content_len = paren_end - paren_start - 1; // -1 to skip opening paren
213       char desc_content[512];
214       strncpy(desc_content, paren_start + 1, desc_content_len); // +1 to skip opening paren
215       desc_content[desc_content_len] = '\0';
216 
217       const char *colored_desc_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, desc_content);
218       char colored_desc_buf[512];
219       strncpy(colored_desc_buf, colored_desc_ptr, sizeof(colored_desc_buf) - 1);
220       colored_desc_buf[sizeof(colored_desc_buf) - 1] = '\0';
221       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
222                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "(%s)", colored_desc_buf);
223 
224       // Now color file:line in parens
225       // Skip leading spaces in file_part
226       const char *file_start = file_part;
227       while (*file_start && *file_start == ' ')
228         file_start++;
229 
230       char *file_colon = strchr(file_start, ':');
231       if (file_colon) {
232         int filename_len = file_colon - file_start;
233         char filename[512];
234         strncpy(filename, file_start, filename_len);
235         filename[filename_len] = '\0';
236 
237         const char *colored_file_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, filename);
238         char colored_file_buf[512];
239         strncpy(colored_file_buf, colored_file_ptr, sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1);
240         colored_file_buf[sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1] = '\0';
241 
242         const char *line_num = file_colon + 1;
243         const char *colored_line_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, line_num);
244         char colored_line_buf[512];
245         strncpy(colored_line_buf, colored_line_ptr, sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1);
246         colored_line_buf[sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1] = '\0';
247 
248         colored_sym_offset +=
249             safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, " (%s:%s)",
250                           colored_file_buf, colored_line_buf);
251       }
252     } else if (paren_start) {
253       // Format: "function() (file:line)"
254       int func_len = paren_start - s;
255       char func_name[512];
256       strncpy(func_name, s, func_len);
257       func_name[func_len] = '\0';
258 
259       const char *colored_func_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEV, func_name);
260       char colored_func_buf[512];
261       strncpy(colored_func_buf, colored_func_ptr, sizeof(colored_func_buf) - 1);
262       colored_func_buf[sizeof(colored_func_buf) - 1] = '\0';
263       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
264                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s()", colored_func_buf);
265 
266       // Find file:line in second set of parens
267       s = paren_start + 1;
268       while (*s && *s != '(')
269         s++;
270 
271       if (*s == '(') {
272         const char *file_paren_end = strchr(s, ')');
273         if (file_paren_end) {
274           colored_sym_offset +=
275               safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, " (");
276           int file_len = file_paren_end - s - 1;
277           char file_part[512];
278           strncpy(file_part, s + 1, file_len);
279           file_part[file_len] = '\0';
280 
281           // Skip leading spaces in file_part
282           const char *file_start = file_part;
283           while (*file_start && *file_start == ' ')
284             file_start++;
285 
286           char *colon_pos = strchr(file_start, ':');
287           if (colon_pos) {
288             int filename_len = colon_pos - file_start;
289             char filename[512];
290             strncpy(filename, file_start, filename_len);
291             filename[filename_len] = '\0';
292 
293             const char *colored_file_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, filename);
294             char colored_file_buf[512];
295             strncpy(colored_file_buf, colored_file_ptr, sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1);
296             colored_file_buf[sizeof(colored_file_buf) - 1] = '\0';
297 
298             const char *line_num = colon_pos + 1;
299             const char *colored_line_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, line_num);
300             char colored_line_buf[512];
301             strncpy(colored_line_buf, colored_line_ptr, sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1);
302             colored_line_buf[sizeof(colored_line_buf) - 1] = '\0';
303 
304             colored_sym_offset +=
305                 safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s:%s",
306                               colored_file_buf, colored_line_buf);
307           }
308           colored_sym_offset +=
309               safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset, sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, ")");
310         }
311       }
312     } else {
313       // No parens, likely a hex address - color with FATAL
314       const char *colored_addr_ptr = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, s);
315       char colored_addr_buf[512];
316       strncpy(colored_addr_buf, colored_addr_ptr, sizeof(colored_addr_buf) - 1);
317       colored_addr_buf[sizeof(colored_addr_buf) - 1] = '\0';
318       colored_sym_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_symbol + colored_sym_offset,
319                                           sizeof(colored_symbol) - colored_sym_offset, "%s", colored_addr_buf);
320     }
321 
322     // Format colored buffer: "  [colored_num] colored_symbol\n"
323     colored_offset += safe_snprintf(colored_buffer + colored_offset, sizeof(colored_buffer) - (size_t)colored_offset,
324                                     "  [%s] %s\n", colored_num_buf, colored_symbol);
325 
326     // Format plain buffer: "  [num] symbol\n"
327     plain_offset += safe_snprintf(plain_buffer + plain_offset, sizeof(plain_buffer) - (size_t)plain_offset,
328                                   "  [%d] %s\n", frame_num, symbol);
329     frame_num++;
330   }
331 
332   // Output backtrace through logging system (no direct fprintf to avoid ANSI codes in captured output)
333   log_warn("%s", plain_buffer);
334   log_file_msg("%s", plain_buffer);
335 }






References asciichat_thread_self, colored_string(),
    backtrace_t::count, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, LOG_COLOR_DEV, LOG_COLOR_ERROR,
    LOG_COLOR_FATAL, LOG_COLOR_GREY, LOG_COLOR_WARN,
    log_file_msg(), log_get_template(),
    log_template_apply(), LOG_WARN, log_warn,
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), safe_snprintf(),
    SET_ERRNO, and backtrace_t::symbols.


Referenced by asciichat_fatal_with_context(),
    asciichat_print_error_context(), backtrace_log_frames(),
    backtrace_print_many(), and backtrace_print_symbols().






void
  backtrace_print_many (const char * label, const backtrace_t * bts, int
  count)


Print multiple backtraces. Convenience function for iterating and
    printing multiple backtrace_t entries.




Parameters


label Header label for all backtraces


bts Array of backtrace structures


count Number of backtraces to print


Definition at line 337 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


337                                                                                 {
338   if (!bts || count <= 0) {
339     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: bts is NULL or count <= 0");
340     return;
341   }
342   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
343     backtrace_print(label, &bts[i], 0, 0, NULL);
344   }
345 }






References backtrace_print(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    and SET_ERRNO.






void
  backtrace_symbolize (backtrace_t * bt)


Symbolize bt->ptrs into bt->symbols. Calls
    platform_backtrace_symbols() to convert raw addresses to symbol
    strings. This is a no-op if symbols are already set (already
  symbolized).




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure to symbolize (must not be
  NULL)


Definition at line 27 of file debug/backtrace.c.


27                                           {
28   if (!bt || bt->tried_symbolize) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid symbolize params");
30     return; // Already tried or invalid (don't try twice)
31   }
32   bt->tried_symbolize = true;
33   bt->symbols = platform_backtrace_symbols(bt->ptrs, bt->count);
34 }






References backtrace_t::count, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    platform_backtrace_symbols(), backtrace_t::ptrs,
    SET_ERRNO, backtrace_t::symbols, and
    backtrace_t::tried_symbolize.


Referenced by asciichat_print_error_context(), and
    backtrace_capture_and_symbolize().






void
  backtrace_t_free (backtrace_t * bt)


Free backtrace symbols. Calls
    platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy() to free the symbols array. Nulls
    the symbols pointer after freeing.




Parameters


bt Backtrace structure (must not be NULL)


Definition at line 45 of file debug/backtrace.c.


45                                        {
46   if (!bt) {
47     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid backtrace params");
48     return;
49   }
50   if (bt->symbols) {
51     platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy(bt->symbols);
52     bt->symbols = NULL;
53   }
54 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    platform_backtrace_symbols_destroy(), SET_ERRNO, and
    backtrace_t::symbols.


Referenced by asciichat_clear_errno(),
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), asciichat_fatal_with_context(),
    asciichat_set_errno(), and backtrace_log_frames().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.11.2
    ascii-chat
